channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said Sunday that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader. “The Reform Party needs a new leader,” Musk said in a post on the social platform X, which he owns. “Farage doesn’t have what it takes.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Latest Samsung Galaxy S25 leak includes predicted specs, pricing, and color details
item.description: Here's what we think might be coming when these phones are unveiled, which should be sometime later this month.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: NASA's Martian helicopter promises unprecedented views of the red planet
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter, which flew on Mars in April 2021, became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on a planet other than Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 hidden reasons you keep running out of iCloud storage
item.description: Apple gives every iCloud user 5GB of free storage space. This storage space can be used for anything—from backing up your iPhone online to saving documents in the cloud to syncing photos across devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: DOJ calls on Supreme Court to deny Trump's request to delay TikTok ban
item.description: The Justice Department urged the Supreme Court on Friday to deny President-elect Trump’s request to postpone their ruling on a TikTok ban set to be implemented the day before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 16 fascinating breakthroughs of 2024 that weren’t about AI
item.description: All the excitement around artificial intelligence can make it hard to notice all the other exciting progress lately that’s been the result of human intelligence. Of course, it’s getting harder to say what’s not got some bit of AI or deep learning in it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Linux filesystems: Ext4, Btrfs, XFS, ZFS and more
item.description: Linux supports quite a few filesystem types. Your choice of which filesystem to use can play an important role in optimizing performance, reliability and data integrity. Among the more popular filesystems are Ext4, Btrfs, XFS and ZFS. Each is widely used and offers some unique features and benefits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk turns Cybertruck explosion into a marketing moment
item.description: The new year started out with the worst kind of bang. In the early hours of January 1, an assailant exploded a Cybertruck in front of Trump Hotel in Las Vegas, using firework mortars and gas canisters.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: It took police more than a week to publicly identify 57-year-old Debrina Kawam as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train on Dec. 22.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Goldin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Working from the office means a pay cut
item.description: In recent months, companies have been forcing workers back into the office with increasing frequency. Annoyingly, many of these are big tech businesses that can most easily enable their workers to work remotely. This trend, likely to continue in 2025, costs workers real money.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Go Developer Survey: Consistent Coding Standards and AI Adoption
item.description: The latest Go developer survey reveals a high level of satisfaction with the programming language, but challenges in maintaining consistent coding standards and a significant adoption of AI assistants.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China hacked Treasury Department earlier this month: Officials
item.description: The bad actor overrode security measures and remotely accessed workstations and "certain unclassified documents" from users.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: How to restore deleted text messages on Android
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals ways to restore accidentally deleted text messages on your Android phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These 6 tech questions were settled in 2024
item.description: The Silicon Valley hype cycle follows a familiar pattern: an emerging technology or tech product or service is hinted at, rumored, leaked, reported on, announced, and then shipped. That’s the cycle for what actually happens.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
